definitions: A unit of temperature on the Celsius scale, equal to one hundredth of the difference between the freezing and boiling points of water.
A unit of temperature where 0 degrees Celsius is the freezing point of water and 100 degrees Celsius is the boiling point of water at standard atmospheric pressure.
examples: Water freezes at zero degree Celsius.
The temperature reached thirty degrees Celsius today.
A comfortable room temperature is around twenty-two degrees Celsius.
